habits.name: Get Morning Sunlight
habits.notes: Step outside, open a window, or sit near light to gently wake your body.

habits.name: Drink Enough Water
habits.notes: Aim for several glasses spread through the day. Keep water visible near you.

habits.name: Workout or Move Your Body
habits.notes: Any intentional movement counts — walk, stretch, dance, or a simple home workout.

habits.name: Declutter Your Space
habits.notes: Pick one drawer, shelf, or small area and bring a bit more order to it.

habits.name: Read Something Daily
habits.notes: Read a few pages of anything that feeds your mind or relaxes you.

habits.name: Write in a Journal
habits.notes: Dump thoughts, reflect on the day, or write a few lines about how you feel.

habits.name: Sleep Before 11 PM
habits.notes: Aim to be in bed with lights off before 11 PM to support deeper rest.

habits.name: Review Monthly Goals
habits.notes: Look at your goals, note what moved forward and what you want to adjust.

habits.name: 5-minutes Stretch Break
habits.notes: Stand up, roll your shoulders, stretch your back and legs gently.

habits.name: Midday Walk Break
habits.notes: Take a short walk, even indoors or around your building, to reset energy.

habits.name: Evening Screen Wind-Down
habits.notes: Put screens away or on do-not-disturb and switch to an offline activity.

habits.name: Plan Tomorrow’s Top 3
habits.notes: Pick the three most important things for tomorrow so you start with clarity.

habits.name: Gratitude Reflection
habits.notes: Write or think of 1–3 things you’re grateful for today.

habits.name: Tidy Your Desk
habits.notes: Clear papers, cups, and random items so tomorrow’s start feels calmer.

habits.name: Digital Cleanup Session
habits.notes: Archive files, close tabs, and delete unused apps or screenshots.

habits.name: Evening Reflection Walk
habits.notes: Take a gentle walk to unwind and mentally review your day.

habits.name: Healthy Snack Choice
habits.notes: Swap one snack for something with more steady energy (fruit, nuts, etc.).

habits.name: Mindful Breathing Break
habits.notes: Pause and take slow breaths, in through the nose and out through the mouth.

habits.name: Step Counter Goal
habits.notes: Set a realistic step target and check it once or twice a day.

habits.name: Prepare Tomorrow’s Water
habits.notes: Fill a bottle or jug so drinking water tomorrow feels effortless.

habits.name: Deep Work Block
habits.notes: Work on one important task with notifications off and no multitasking.

habits.name: Inbox Zero Mini
habits.notes: Archive, delete, or reply to enough emails to feel a bit more caught up.

habits.name: Daily Task Review
habits.notes: Review what you did today, check off tasks, and move the rest forward.

habits.name: Check In With Feelings
habits.notes: Name how you’re feeling and why, without judging yourself.

habits.name: Small Act of Kindness
habits.notes: Do one small kind thing for someone else or for future-you.

habits.name: Start Bedtime Routine
habits.notes: Begin a simple routine: wash up, dim lights, prepare clothes, and unplug slowly.

habits.name: No Caffeine After Evening
habits.notes: Avoid coffee/energy drinks later in the day to support easier sleep.

habits.name: Weekly Room Reset
habits.notes: Reset your main room: make the bed, clear surfaces, and put things back.

habits.name: Organise Tomorrow’s Bag
habits.notes: Pack your bag or prepare your workspace so tomorrow morning feels smoother.

habits.name: Weekly Learning Session
habits.notes: Spend time learning or practising one skill or topic you care about.

habits.name: Sunday Reflection & Planning
habits.notes: Look back at the week, note wins and lessons, and loosely plan the next one.

habits.name: Morning Body Activation
habits.notes: Gently loosen your joints every morning with light mobility.

habits.name: Balanced Breakfast Choice
habits.notes: Choose a simple breakfast with protein and fiber for steadier energy.

habits.name: Afternoon Energy Check
habits.notes: Check whether you need water, rest, food, or movement.

habits.name: Post-Lunch Mini Reset
habits.notes: Stand up and move lightly for one minutes after eating.

habits.name: Micro Strength Set
habits.notes: Do a tiny strength set twice a week to build momentum.

habits.name: Healthy Evening Snack
habits.notes: Choose a light, digestion-friendly snack at least once a week.

habits.name: Two-minutes Task Start
habits.notes: Begin any difficult task with just two committed minutes.

habits.name: Focus Timer Cycle
habits.notes: Use a cycle of 10 minutes focus and 2 minutes rest.

habits.name: Daily Goal Visualization
habits.notes: Visualize your top outcome for the day with clarity.

habits.name: Micro Inbox Cleanup
habits.notes: Clear a small batch of messages weekly to reduce overload.

habits.name: Single-Tab Focus
habits.notes: Keep only one tab open for your current task to stay present.

habits.name: Midday Refocus Pause
habits.notes: A tiny mid-day reset to restore clarity.

habits.name: Emotional Labeling
habits.notes: Pause and name your emotion to reduce intensity.

habits.name: Self-Compassion Note
habits.notes: Write a gentle sentence of kindness toward yourself.

habits.name: Mini Mind-Clear Dump
habits.notes: Write down three thoughts that feel heavy this week.

habits.name: Slow Exhale Practice
habits.notes: Do slow, long exhales to calm your nervous system.

habits.name: Micro Enjoyment Moment
habits.notes: Pause and enjoy a pleasant moment around you.

habits.name: Consistent Wake Alarm
habits.notes: Use the same wake-up time to stabilize your body clock.

habits.name: Lights-Dimmed Signal
habits.notes: Dim lights weekly as a gentle evening reminder ritual.

habits.name: No Phone for 5 minutes
habits.notes: Avoid your phone for your first five minutes awake.

habits.name: Evening Bedroom Reset
habits.notes: Reset your sleeping space once a week for calm nights.

habits.name: Warm Shower Wind-Down
habits.notes: Take a warm shower to help your body relax and prepare for rest.

habits.name: One-minutes Room Reset
habits.notes: Do a quick room tidy to keep things manageable.

habits.name: Micro Digital Sort
habits.notes: Declutter a few files or photos weekly to stay organized.

habits.name: Fridge Quick Check
habits.notes: Scan for expired or unneeded items to keep things tidy.

habits.name: Workspace End Reset
habits.notes: Reset your desk by placing three items back where they belong.

habits.name: Declutter Entry Area
habits.notes: Keep your entry area clear to create a sense of calm at home.

habits.name: One Page Learning
habits.notes: Read one page of something helpful or inspiring.

habits.name: Tiny Identity Statement
habits.notes: Say one sentence reinforcing the person you're becoming.

habits.name: Weekly Mini Reflection
habits.notes: Reflect briefly on what felt meaningful this week.

habits.name: Gratitude to Others
habits.notes: Share gratitude with someone to strengthen connection.

questions.text: To make this feel personal, which area do you most want to improve right now?
Right now, how would you describe your overall energy and health?

options.label: I feel low or drained most days
I’m okay but crash at certain times
I feel mostly fine, just want to optimize

questions.text: When is your low energy most noticeable?

options.label: I feel tired pretty much all day

questions.text: When do you usually feel that energy crash?
What would you most like to improve about your energy?
What do your mornings usually look like?

options.label: I check my phone in bed and move slowly
I rush out with no time for myself
I feel heavy even if I sleep enough

questions.text: What’s your afternoon usually like?

options.label: I sit for long periods without moving
I lose focus and scroll a lot

questions.text: When you feel tired all day, what do you notice most?

options.label: My body feels heavy or sore
Both body and mind feel sluggish

questions.text: What best describes your usual meals before you crash?

options.label: Heavy or fast food most days
I often skip or eat very little
It’s random and changes a lot

questions.text: What’s happening around the time you usually crash?

options.label: I’ve been sitting and working for hours
I’ve been on my phone a lot
I’ve barely eaten or drunk water

questions.text: How do your evenings usually feel?

options.label: I just want to collapse on the couch
I keep working or studying late
I zone out on screens for hours

questions.text: How many days per week could you realistically move your body?
Where do you want more stamina or consistency?
What feels most important to adjust first?
For morning changes, what feels realistic right now?
For your afternoon energy, what kind of change feels doable?
What feels like the most supportive starting point?
What kind of first step around meals feels right?

options.label: Tiny change to what I eat

questions.text: What support would help with late-day crashes?
What would you most like your evenings to feel like?
What style of movement sounds most like you?
What kind of stamina habit feels best to start with?
Which feels easiest to shape into a habit?
What type of tiny morning habit sounds best for you?
For your afternoon energy, what sounds most supportive?
For feeling tired all day, where should we start?
What change around meals feels most realistic right now?

options.label: A walk or stretch after eating

questions.text: For late-day crashes, what sounds like the best first move?

options.label: Short reset breaks during the day

questions.text: What kind of evening would feel best for you?
How would you like your movement habit to feel?
What would help you show up more consistently?
What kind of food and water support feels best?
Tiny morning movement to gently wake up your body.
A calm morning start with light and hydration.
Prepare the night before so mornings feel lighter.
Small movement breaks to fight afternoon heaviness.
Short walks to refresh your afternoon energy.
Support your afternoon energy with better fuel.
Reset your all-day tiredness with better sleep anchors.
Gentle daily movement to support your whole day.
Light and hydration to lift your all-day energy.
Support your post-meal energy with tiny food upgrades.
Movement around meals to smooth out crashes.
Hydration that supports more stable post-meal energy.
Tiny resets during the day to prevent late crashes.
Afternoon movement to keep energy more stable.
A gentler evening wind-down to support your energy.
Make evenings feel lighter and less draining.
More calm and softness in your evenings.
More intentional and structured nights.
Light, accessible movement to support your fitness.
Stronger movement habits for a bigger fitness boost.
Movement woven into your day in simple ways.
Small daily check-ins to support steady stamina.
Weekly adjustments to keep your energy on track.
Plan your days around energy, not just tasks.
Gentle snack changes to support your energy.
A structured hydration rhythm for your day.
Balanced food and water habits over time.
How do you feel about your focus and productivity recently?

options.label: I feel scattered and often behind
I get things done but it feels stressful
I’m mostly okay but want to focus better

questions.text: When you think about feeling scattered, what feels most true?

options.label: I have too many tasks and feel lost
I don’t really have a clear system or routine

questions.text: What makes your productivity feel stressful?

options.label: I rarely switch off from work or study

questions.text: If your focus improved, what would you notice first?

options.label: I’d be less distracted and more present
My work or study would feel more intentional
I’d make clearer progress on important things

questions.text: What usually pulls your attention away the most?
When you have many tasks, what happens first?

options.label: I jump between tasks without finishing
I start working but feel chaotic

questions.text: What do you currently use to manage tasks or study?

options.label: I don’t really use any system
I use something sometimes but not consistently
I plan a lot but still feel disorganised

questions.text: When you feel rushed, what is your day usually like?

options.label: I start the day without a clear plan
I know my tasks but there are too many
I say yes to too many things

questions.text: What makes it hard to switch off from work or study?

options.label: I keep checking messages or emails
I work or study right up until sleep
I feel guilty when I’m not working

questions.text: Where do you feel that exhaustion the most?

options.label: In my whole life (no space for me)

questions.text: When do you most wish you could stay focused?

options.label: During deep work or study sessions
During both deep and light work

questions.text: What would make your work or study feel more intentional?
Where do you most want to see better progress?

options.label: Both daily tasks and big goals

questions.text: For your focus, what kind of support feels easiest to start with?
What kind of planning structure sounds most helpful?

options.label: Daily priorities I can actually finish
Connecting daily tasks to bigger goals

questions.text: For deeper focus, what feels like the right starting point?

options.label: Clear shutdown so my brain can rest

questions.text: We’ll support you with light, simple clarity each day.
A tiny daily review to feel more on top of things.
A clearer space to give your mind more room.
Daily priorities you can actually finish.
A weekly reset so you feel more in control.
Connect your everyday tasks with your bigger goals.
Create space for deep, focused work.
Stronger boundaries with screens to protect your focus.
A clearer shutdown so your mind can rest and reset.
Overall, how have you been feeling emotionally lately?

options.label: Stressed or overwhelmed a lot of the time
Up and down, my mood swings around
Mostly flat, low motivation or numb

questions.text: When you feel stressed or overwhelmed, what seems like the biggest source?

options.label: People or relationships in my life
Work/study is spilling into all of my time

questions.text: When your mood goes up and down, what do you wish you had more of?

options.label: More moments of feeling proud of myself

questions.text: When you feel flat or low motivation, what kind of small step sounds most reachable?

options.label: Getting some light or fresh air
Writing a few lines to clear my head

questions.text: When there are too many tasks, how do you usually respond?

options.label: I push through and ignore how I feel
I get tense and my body feels on edge
I avoid tasks and distract myself

questions.text: With people or relationships, what feels hardest?

options.label: I feel drained and need more calm time
I wish I had more positive moments with people

questions.text: When work or study spills into your life, what happens most?

options.label: I don’t stop to notice how I feel
My body and mind feel wired and restless
I lose all time for small enjoyable moments

questions.text: When your mood swings, when do you most wish for calm?

options.label: In quiet moments, to understand myself
During the day, when things feel intense

questions.text: What would help you feel more proud of yourself?

options.label: Seeing my thoughts and wins on paper
Feeling calmer and more in control of my day
Doing small kind or meaningful actions

questions.text: What kind of ‘more fun’ sounds most supportive right now?

options.label: Gentle, reflective fun (like creative journaling or reading)
Calm and grounding fun (like walks or relaxing moments)

questions.text: When you imagine moving your body a bit more, what feels most approachable?

options.label: Reflecting first, then a tiny bit of movement
A calm, gentle walk or stretch
Making it feel like a small, enjoyable action

questions.text: When you think of light and fresh air, what draws you most?

options.label: Using that time to think and reflect
Using it as a calm reset break
Using it as a small moment of joy

questions.text: If you wrote a few lines, what would you want from that moment?

options.label: To calm down and soften the day
To notice small good or kind things

questions.text: For reflection, what kind of support feels best to start with?

options.label: A simple gratitude check each day

questions.text: For calm and grounding, what sounds most supportive?
For more joy and lightness, what feels like the right place to begin?

options.label: Tiny bits of enjoyable reading or learning

questions.text: We’ll support you with a small journaling habit to organise your thoughts and emotions.
Let’s bring in gentle gratitude to shift how your days feel.
We’ll mix tiny reflection moments to help you stay in touch with yourself.
Short breathing breaks can give your mind and body gentle calm.
Calm walks and gentle movement can help release some of the stress you carry.
Better wind-down and rest can quietly transform how you feel during the day.
Small acts of kindness can gently lift your mood and sense of connection.
Light, enjoyable learning can bring more interest and colour into your days.
We’ll focus on gentle, connecting moments that make life feel warmer.
How would you describe your sleep and daily routine right now?

options.label: Mostly okay, I just want a smoother flow

questions.text: When you say your sleep is a mess, what feels like the core issue?

options.label: Both going to bed late and waking up tired

questions.text: What usually keeps you up or disrupts your sleep the most?

options.label: I just lose track of time

questions.text: What feels like the easiest place to start improving your sleep?

options.label: A simple wind-down routine before bed
Being more consistent with my bedtime
Making my mornings feel more supportive

questions.text: Let’s start with a gentle wind-down so your body knows it’s time to rest.
We’ll focus on a clearer, more consistent bedtime.
We’ll support your mornings so waking up feels a bit easier.
When you say things are inconsistent, what changes the most?

options.label: What time I go to sleep

questions.text: What would you like to focus on first to bring more consistency?
How big of a change feels realistic for you right now?

options.label: Two small anchors I can repeat

questions.text: We’ll start with one strong anchor for your sleep.
Two simple anchors to gently stabilise your routine.
A slightly stronger routine to hold your days together.
Things are mostly okay — where would you like a bit more ease?
What feels most missing from your current routine?

options.label: A bit more energy and momentum

questions.text: What kind of approach sounds most helpful to you right now?

options.label: Light planning so my days feel clearer
Reflection so I can process the day

questions.text: Simple planning habits can make your days feel smoother and more intentional.
Gentle reflection can help you close the day with more peace.
Small rituals can turn mornings and evenings into supportive anchors.
How does your space and organisation feel right now?

options.label: My goals and plans feel messy

questions.text: When you think about physical clutter, what area bothers you most?
How do you usually feel when you notice the clutter?

options.label: Overwhelmed and don’t know where to start
Numb, I ignore it until it’s really bad

questions.text: What kind of change feels most realistic for you right now?

options.label: Tiny daily actions to slowly clear things
A weekly deeper reset for my space
A mix of small daily steps and weekly reset

questions.text: We’ll start with tiny daily actions so your space gets clearer without feeling overwhelming.
A weekly reset will help you create a calmer physical environment over time.
We’ll use both tiny daily steps and a weekly reset to support your space and your mind.
When you think about digital clutter, what feels like the biggest pain?

options.label: Files and folders (on my phone or computer)

questions.text: When does your digital clutter bother you the most?

options.label: In the morning when I first check my phone
At night when I want to wind down

questions.text: What kind of cleanup rhythm sounds most helpful?

options.label: A mix of weekly and daily cleanups

questions.text: We’ll use weekly digital cleanups to keep things from piling up too much.
Tiny daily cleanups will help your digital world feel lighter and more under control.
We’ll combine daily and weekly cleanups so your digital life stays manageable.
When you think about your goals and plans, what feels most unclear?

options.label: My goals themselves (what I actually want)
The steps to reach my goals
How to track or review my progress

questions.text: Which time horizon feels most important for you right now?
What kind of support would feel best for your goals and plans?

options.label: Writing and reflection to understand myself and my goals
Light planning and review so things feel organised
A stronger planning structure to stay on track

questions.text: Gentle reflection will help you clarify what matters and how you feel about it.
Light planning and review will help your goals feel more organised and doable.
We’ll build a stronger structure to connect your days to your bigger goals.
How do you feel about your personal growth right now?

options.label: I’m doing okay but want more direction

questions.text: When you feel stuck, where do you notice it most?

options.label: Understanding myself and who I am

questions.text: Right now, what kind of growth step feels realistically possible?

options.label: A mix of both daily and weekly

questions.text: What kind of activity sounds most helpful for you to get unstuck?

options.label: Writing and reflecting on my thoughts

questions.text: We’ll support you with gentle learning habits to help you move out of feeling stuck.
Writing and reflection can help you understand yourself and your direction more clearly.
Small experiments and regular check-ins can help you discover what works for you.
When you think about growing inconsistently, what usually breaks your consistency?
When does it feel easiest to invest a little in your growth?
What kind of support would help you stay more consistent?
We’ll use small, repeatable steps so growth fits more easily into your days.
Weekly reflections can help you see your growth and adjust gently.
A simple routine will help you keep moving toward the person you want to become.
When you think about wanting more direction, what feels most missing?

options.label: Clarity about my values or who I am
Regular time to review and adjust

questions.text: For your direction, which time horizon feels most important right now?
What kind of support would help you feel more guided and on track?

options.label: Writing prompts to explore my thoughts
A mix of reflection and planning

questions.text: We’ll use writing to help you clarify your direction and what matters most to you.
Planning and tracking habits will help you turn your direction into concrete steps.
We’ll combine reflection and planning so you can adjust your path as you grow.
What best describes your current focus and productivity situation?

options.label: I’m not clear what to prioritise
I plan a lot but still feel behind

questions.text: When you lose focus, what usually distracts you the most?

options.label: People around me or my environment

questions.text: When does phone or social media distraction hit you the most?

options.label: In the morning when I start my day
In the evening when I want to wind down

questions.text: What feels like a realistic first step to protect your focus from your phone?

options.label: A tiny time limit (5–10 minutes off my phone)
A short block of focused time with my phone away

questions.text: We’ll start with a light social detox to protect your attention without feeling extreme.
Focused blocks with your phone away can help you make real progress.
An evening screen detox can calm your mind and support better focus the next day.
When do people or your environment distract you the most?

options.label: When I sit at my desk or workspace
When I’m working or studying in shared spaces

questions.text: What kind of support would help you stay focused around people and noise?

options.label: Quick calm-down moments when I feel pulled away

questions.text: A tidier workspace can make it easier to ignore distractions around you.
We’ll use focused work blocks and gentle breaks to protect your attention.
Short calm moments can help you reset even in a busy environment.
When do your own thoughts and worries distract you most?

options.label: At the end of the day when I reflect

questions.text: What would help your mind feel more supported during focus time?

options.label: A quick breathing or grounding habit
Writing things down before or after work
A mix of small planning and calm moments

questions.text: Short breathing breaks can help your mind feel less scattered.
Writing your thoughts down can give them a home so you can focus more clearly.
A bit of planning and a bit of calm can help your mind feel more grounded.
When you think about priorities, what feels most true?

options.label: I have too many tasks and don’t know what to do first
I’m not sure what actually matters most
Everything feels urgent all the time

questions.text: How do you currently plan your day or week, if at all?

options.label: I plan sometimes but don’t stick to it
I plan a lot but still feel behind

questions.text: What do you most want your planning to give you?

options.label: Daily clarity about my most important tasks
A weekly overview so I see the bigger picture
Both daily clarity and a weekly overview

questions.text: We’ll keep it simple and build daily clarity into your routine.
A weekly view will help you feel more on top of your priorities.
We’ll combine daily clarity with a weekly overview so you feel more in control.
Where do you mostly feel overloaded or like you’re always behind?

options.label: Both work and study or multiple roles

questions.text: What do you feel would help you most with that overloaded feeling?

options.label: A clearer shutdown ritual at the end of the day
A stronger weekly reset so I start fresh

questions.text: How big of a change feels realistic for you right now?

options.label: Tiny tweaks to how I work
A stronger reset for endings of my day
Trying both daily tweaks and weekly reset

questions.text: Focused blocks with small reviews can help you feel less scattered and more effective.
A clear shutdown can help you leave work or study in its place.
Weekly resets and small cleanups can make your next week feel lighter.
How have you been feeling emotionally lately?
When you feel stressed or overwhelmed, what is the main source?
When stress shows up, how do you usually respond?

options.label: I keep pushing through and ignore it
I shut down or scroll on my phone
I vent or complain but still feel stuck

questions.text: What feels like the safest place to start right now?

options.label: Private reflection and getting thoughts out
Body-based calm and short grounding moments
Gentle planning so things feel more organised

questions.text: Reflection can give your feelings a safe place to land and help you feel less crowded inside.
Short calm moments can help your body and mind step out of survival mode more often.
Gentle planning habits can make your days feel more manageable and less overwhelming.
With your mood going up and down, what would you like more of in your days?

options.label: More pride and sense of progress

questions.text: When do these mood swings show up the most?
What kind of habit style feels most natural to you?

options.label: Small, concrete actions I can complete
Moments that bring joy or lightness

questions.text: Calm check-ins can help your mood feel less like a rollercoaster and more like gentle waves.
Small wins and reviews can help you end more days feeling proud of yourself.
Tiny moments of kindness and interest can bring more joy into your days.
When your mood feels flat or your motivation is low, what feels most reachable right now?

options.label: Getting some light or fresh air
Writing a few lines about my day

questions.text: How often do you feel you could realistically try that?

options.label: Only on specific days I choose

questions.text: How much energy do you want this habit to take at the beginning?

options.label: I’ll try and see what feels right

questions.text: Gentle movement can be a soft restart for low motivation days.
Light and fresh air can give your mood a quiet lift.
Writing a few lines can help you reconnect with yourself, even on low days.
